findings.detail: Description of "inspect_requests" references sensitive artifacts (credentials, secret, cookie). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "summarize_device_traffic" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

description: Get a 5-minute signed download URL for a snapshot file (name from list_snapshots).

labels.name: list_push_tokens
labels.description: List registered APNs push tokens (masked). Optionally scope to one device.

labels.name: get_push_response
labels.description: Read back a device's answer to an actionable push (#75) by the correlation_id returned from send_push. Returns the push_responses row { correlation_id, device_uuid, action (accept|reject|skip), context, responded_at, action_intent } or null if the user hasn't responded yet. action_intent (or null) c

labels.name: get_stats
labels.description: Return the live stats snapshot (get_stats RPC).

labels.name: get_status
labels.description: Return the latest infra status snapshot (service_status) MERGED with the Edge tier's self-reported deployed build. findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp.busymate.dev" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: list_devices, get_my_account, search_entries, inspect_requests, share_advisor_finding, download_snapshot, get_status · Private data: get_device, get_my_account, inspect_requests, list_snapshots, download_snapshot, list_push_tokens, get_status, list_audit_events, get_audit_event · Public sink: get_my_account, share_advisor_finding, list_audit_events · Destructive: share_advisor_finding, get_status.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: list_devices, get_my_account, search_entries, inspect_requests, share_advisor_finding, download_snapshot, get_status · Private data: get_device, get_my_account, inspect_requests, list_snapshots, download_snapshot, list_push_tokens, get_status, list_audit_events, get_audit_event · Public sink: get_my_account, share_advisor_finding, list_audit_events · Destructive: share_advisor_finding, get_status

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
